# UC-20.3.6 · License Pool Allocation Optimization

## Description

Shifting unused pool capacity between departments or regions avoids buying new seats while one pool sits idle.

## Value

Shifting unused pool capacity between departments or regions avoids buying new seats while one pool sits idle.

## Implementation

Ingest per–cost-center assignments against shared enterprise pools. Identify negative slack (overallocation) and large positive slack (reclaimable). Recommend transfers using simple optimization rules in a lookup updated monthly.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: ServiceNow SAM, custom pool allocator.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=license:usage`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest per–cost-center assignments against shared enterprise pools. Identify negative slack (overallocation) and large positive slack (reclaimable). Recommend transfers using simple optimization rules in a lookup updated monthly.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=saas sourcetype="license:usage"
| stats sum(assigned) as assigned sum(consumed) as consumed by pool_id, org_unit
| eval slack=assigned-consumed
| eventstats sum(slack) as total_slack by pool_id
| where slack < 0 OR (slack > 50 AND total_slack > 0)
| sort pool_id, slack
```

Understanding this SPL

**License Pool Allocation Optimization** — Shifting unused pool capacity between departments or regions avoids buying new seats while one pool sits idle.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=license:usage`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): ServiceNow SAM, custom pool allocator. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: saas; **sourcetype**: license:usage. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=saas, sourcetype="license:usage". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by pool_id, org_unit** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **slack** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by pool_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where slack < 0 OR (slack > 50 AND total_slack > 0)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
compare with ServiceNow ITAM; compare with the cloud billing console for the same scope, accounts, and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap (org × pool utilization), Table (rebalance suggestions), Bar chart (slack by pool).

## SPL

```spl
index=saas sourcetype="license:usage"
| stats sum(assigned) as assigned sum(consumed) as consumed by pool_id, org_unit
| eval slack=assigned-consumed
| eventstats sum(slack) as total_slack by pool_id
| where slack < 0 OR (slack > 50 AND total_slack > 0)
| sort pool_id, slack
```

## Visualization

Heatmap (org × pool utilization), Table (rebalance suggestions), Bar chart (slack by pool).
